claims.expected_trend: Increasing static disorder suppresses spatial delocalization in tight-binding-like networks.
claims.reason: Participation falls and IPR rises with disorder in multiple families.

claims.expected_trend: Experiments can show best transport when disorder and dephasing are balanced.
claims.threshold: persistent positive dephasing gain with high-noise suppression
claims.reason: The effective model reproduces the qualitative balanced-regime motif, not the perovskite microscopic experiment.

claims.expected_trend: A perovskite experiment requires material-specific structure, temperature dependence, and exciton parameters.
claims.reason: This lab uses controlled effective networks and does not yet include perovskite nanocrystal parameters.

claims.expected_trend: Noise can improve excitation transfer through a dissipative network instead of only degrading it.
claims.reason: The sink/loss model contains cases where nonzero dephasing improves useful arrival.

claims.expected_trend: Some disordered networks can have more than one optimal noise regime rather than a single Goldilocks peak.
claims.threshold: at least two separated local maxima
claims.reason: Gamma-resolved curves did not show two resolved local maxima in this profile.

claims.expected_trend: Noisy quantum-network dynamics should be compared against classical and topological controls before claiming a quantum signature.
claims.reason: The suite exports noisy quantum, classical-control, and topology-sensitive comparisons.

claims.expected_trend: Spectroscopic experiments can reveal coherent excitation dynamics in photosynthetic complexes.
claims.threshold: microscopic photosynthetic complex and spectroscopy required
claims.reason: This paper motivates coherent excitation transport but is not reproduced by a generic graph model.

claims.expected_trend: Interacting two-particle quantum walks can distinguish graph structures beyond some single-particle invariants.
claims.reason: The current lab intentionally stays in the single-excitation sector, so this paper is a limitation guardrail rather than a direct benchmark.

claims.expected_trend: Moderate decoherence can tune quantum-walk spreading or mixing, while excessive decoherence removes coherent advantages.
claims.reason: The simulations show a useful nonzero-dephasing region and a high-dephasing penalty.

claims.expected_trend: Finite controlled networks can show coherent, assisted, and high-noise-damped regimes.
claims.threshold: finite N and dephasing window with suppression
claims.reason: The effective finite-network model reproduces the qualitative regime progression.

claims.expected_trend: Exact trapped-ion/qubit hardware reproduction would require microscopic hardware parameters.
claims.reason: This lab tests a qualitative finite-network analogue, not the experimental hardware implementation.

claims.expected_trend: Stationary transport should be measured with a source-drain steady state, not with the absorbing finite-time sink model.
claims.reason: A separate trace-preserving source-drain NESS benchmark returns finite validated current.

claims.expected_trend: Quantum-walk dynamic signatures should classify graph families above baseline.
claims.reason: Dynamic and combined signatures beat the group-split baseline.

claims.expected_trend: Intermediate environment action improves sink efficiency, while strongest dephasing is not always optimal.
claims.reason: Best transport occurs at nonzero dephasing and high dephasing is not uniformly optimal.

claims.expected_trend: Closed CTQW observables depend on network topology.
claims.reason: Closed-system return differs across topologies while numerical closure is valid.

claims.expected_trend: Moderate disorder can improve transport in suboptimal regimes.
claims.reason: At least one matched context improves with nonzero disorder.

claims.expected_trend: Nonzero dephasing can improve useful target arrival.
claims.reason: A nonzero dephasing point has gain above threshold with positive CI95 lower bound.

claims.expected_trend: Changing only trap/target position changes transport efficiency.
claims.reason: Target placement produces efficiency spread above threshold.

claims.expected_trend: Target degree alone should not explain transport efficiency.
claims.reason: Degree explains less than the threshold fraction of target-arrival variation.

claims.expected_trend: Efficiency maps contain a useful intermediate dephasing window across disorder values.
claims.reason: Positive dephasing gain persists across disorder values and high-noise suppression appears.

claims.expected_trend: Fractal lattices can show anomalous spreading governed by geometry and spectral structure.
claims.threshold: at least two changed fractal-vs-lattice comparisons
claims.reason: Fractal benchmarks differ from the square-lattice control in multiple spreading comparisons.

claims.expected_trend: Dynamic CTQW-inspired signatures should place same-family graphs closer than different-family graphs.
claims.reason: Inter-family dynamic-signature distance is larger than intra-family distance.

claims.expected_trend: Disordered noisy networks should be evaluated by ensemble mean, variance, and topology sensitivity, not a single best curve.
claims.reason: The benchmark exports ensemble spread and at least one statistically resolved noisy-transport gain.

claims.expected_trend: Quantum/open signatures should not be fully explained by the classical rate-walk control.
claims.reason: Quantum/open signatures classify better than the classical-control features.
